title: Olthoo's Utilities
description: A personal client-side modpack focused on performance, quality of life, and essential tools for server admins — fully vanilla and server-compatible.
body:
---  

**My Personal Client-Side Modpack for Server Admins**  

This is the **personal client-side modpack I use** for managing and playing on Minecraft servers. It's built to **boost performance, improve quality of life, and give essential tools as a server admin**, all while staying **fully compatible with vanilla servers**.  

---  

## ✅ What This Pack Does  

### ⚙️ Performance & Optimization  
- **Massive FPS boosts**, smoother chunk loading, and **reduced stutter**.  
- Lower memory usage and improved stability for long play sessions.  
- Smarter rendering to **avoid unnecessary lag** and improve client responsiveness.  

### 🎮 Visual & UI Enhancements  
- Clean **32x textures** and **shader support** to make the game look great without overloading performance.  
- **Dark mode menus**, better HUD overlays, zoom functionality, and fullscreen advancements to make managing servers and gameplay easier and more comfortable.  

### 🧰 Admin Tools & Gameplay Utilities  
- **Inventory management tools** for quick sorting and organization.  
- In-game **HUD and debug overlays** to help monitor server activity and issues.  
- **Camera controls and freecam** for better observation and admin work.  

### 🔧 Quality of Life  
- **Toggleable brightness**, better entity and block rendering, and **dark loading screens**.  
- **Faster login and quit times** — perfect when hopping between servers.   
- **Live account switch** — change account without relaunching the game.  
- **Performance-focused background tweaks** to keep gameplay smooth even when multitasking.  

### 🔑 Server Admin Essentials  
- Tools that help me **monitor, debug, and manage** servers directly from my client.  
---  

## 🛡️ Server Friendly  
- **100% client-side**, meaning nothing needs to be installed on a server.  
- Fully compatible with **vanilla and modded servers** — I use it on both without issues.  

---
